rationale: The homes in this Humble, TX suburban neighborhood feature predominantly hip-style roofs with moderate visual characteristics consistent with a 6:12 pitch. Ground shadows cast from the eave edges extend roughly 1–1.5× the overhang width, indicating a moderate but not steep slope. The hip roof planes are clearly visible from the near-nadir view but are not dramatically foreshortened (which would indicate steeper pitch) nor nearly flat-looking (which would indicate shallower pitch). The ridge is noticeably inset from the eave perimeter but occupies a moderate proportion of the footprint. Regionally, Houston-area suburban construction from the 2000s–2010s strongly favors 6:12 as the standard design pitch, and all visual cues here align with that estimate.
